reviews.review_description: First off This game is amazing but why hasnt there been any content updates or a new game i know your small dev team or 1 i think but pls release a conquest mode or maybe a new mission orrrrr a new game

developer_response.body: Honest answer, I just got a second kid. Lmk what you'd like to see. Once I have more time ill roll out an update

reviews.review_description:
It's one of those games which make you hooked from the first few minutes. 
The controls and weapons are awesome. When it comes to graphics, they are simple, but stylish. The music is a great techno playlist. 
Oh, there is also a story, for those who want it.
Me? Too busy blasting the enemies, it's incredibly fun.
P.S. It is a solo dev project. Mind-blown.
If you open your menu at the same time as you die it allows the game to continue and finish the level, but that aside I love this game wish the was an open wold version

developer_response.body:
I wish i could say thats a feature not a bug, lol.

I'll make a mental note to look into that.

An Open World version of this game would probably have to be a sequel tbh

reviews.review_description:
I’m going to leave a small review based after playing once, I’m a huge space fan so my expectations were high after seeing the trailer, graphics are OK, could be better, I am saying this as a developer and I am fully aware the capabilities of what can be achieved on standalone VR devices.

Bad points:
The Cockpits seem to be very large, was expecting them to be fighter size cockpits from the trailer.
The position of the throttle is way too far back, almost impossible to interact with.
Enemy ships are too slow, you need to be at a standstill to shoot them.
Voice acting is clearly AI generated and is absolutely awful.
Audio is so annoying, music and SFX. 

Good points: 
Game is still in development so hopefully we see large improvements 
Another space game is a good thing.
Environments are quite large.

I wish the developer will on the development of this spaceship game

developer_response.body:
Hi Damo. Thanks for the feedback. I will admit, the ships originally had much smaller cockpits, but early in development, there were issues with the AI being able to fit such small ships, so the player ships, and their cockpits got scaled up to make the (!!). This was circa 2020, so I've since felt it too late to roll back this decision, despite fixing the original AI issues. The Meteor ship which is unlockable midway through the game has a much tighter cockpit, which is closer to the original vision of the game.

The throttle might be easier to reach if you recenter your cockpit. Unforunately Meta made it infinitely more difficult to do this with recent OS updates :(. When this game released, recentering was done by holding down the left oculus button.

All in all, life and career priorities have made it more difficult to polish this game, but I will try to incrementally keep making progress on graphics, UI, and translations. Thank you for your review, and giving this game a 3 star review versus Space Salvage's 1 star review ;)

reviews.review_description: Smooth reactive flight controls, interactive immersion, I am in the cockpit, flying, fighting, saving the galaxy - I love this game !

reviews.review_title: good game, small problem on control
reviews.review_description:
Good game overall. Simple graphic but smooth motion.  the space and ship control design is good. Only problem at present is virtual stick's pitch control in Aero mode. Only one direction . May be a bug I don't know. Pls kindly fix it in coming update. So  the only way is using the physical joystick from controller to control the pitch. But I prefer using virtual stick.
Update : thanks for the quick response from developer. I aware he/she is sole development.  Wish your best work from coming update. I revise to five star from four. It is really a good game and has much of fun. Developer have heart to listen and improve. thanks

developer_response.body: The Aero control scheme tracks rotation and not position. So try rotating your wrists forwards and backwards. Apologies if this was not clear from the tutorial. I will try to make this clearer in a future update. If this was helpful please feel free to bump up the review score as it would help out a lot!

reviews.review_description: At first glance, this game may seem very simple. But in reality, behind its straightforward mechanics lies an engaging space action experience with a dramatic storyline, where you need to strategize, manage your ship's resources, and come up with tactics for different enemy types. One of the most thrilling VR games I've ever played.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the positive vibes!

reviews.review_title: Best VR space combat game I have played
reviews.review_description: This game is a fantastic love letter to the wing commander games.  It is incredible that a single developer made this game and this one deserves more support.  The flight model and combat is intuitive and, most importantly, an absolute blast. Graphics are fine and achieve everything as intended and the interactive cockpits are really well done. I played rogue squadron a lot and the best way I can praise this game is to honestly say that I prefer this one hands down. For feedback, I would love a more stirring soundtrack and effects, but this is no criticism as dev, you are doing an amazing job and I can't wait to see how this one continues to develop.

This is one of the greatest games I've played on the quest. The physics/mechanics are incredible and I find the game very immersive. It's like kind of like star wars squadrons. Can't believe this game isn't more popular, hope the devs can spread the word

developer_response.body: Thanks for the positive review. I'm actually a solodev

reviews.review_description: Very encouraged by this Early Access release.  So very reminiscent of the classic PC games like Wing Commander, X-Wing vs. Tie Fighter and Elite.  Very much hoping this developer gets the support he needs to continue expanding on this!  Control scheme is much better than expected - full 3D maneuvering can be difficult to come to grips with, but Rogue does a good job making it easy.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! More updates will be trickling out soon!

Did you get to the end of the game?
- Thanks for pointing out the audio settings issue! This was fixed in the latest update
- The lack of shaking was purposeful to avoid motion sickness. Based on your feedback  the ship now throws sparks in the cockpit when hit. Will add caption feedback in a future update
- Parking inside the enemy shields should be somewhat hard to pull off IMO hence the slow reverse. Have been thinking about adding a hard reverse thruster to one of the ships, but haven't decide on which one.

Have a nice day!

reviews.review_description: Did not think the game would be this good ( i was wrong )

First I was confused and frustrated about the steering method. The stick seems not to move. After a hint from the developer I tested again. Beware that you have to steer with the rotation of your hand instead of moving the virtual stick.

The game is strongly reminiscent of Wing Commander from the nineties and that is meant in a positive way.

In the linear mission progression, you have to complete various tasks and have different spaceships at your disposal, from reconnaissance aircraft to bombers, which have different strengths and weaknesses.

The graphics are kept rather simple, but the interaction in the cockpit is better than in some commercial games. For example, there is a fire extinguisher that you have to use to put out fires if the spaceship is badly damaged. Meanwhile, of course, you must continue to steer. To activate the secondary weapons you have to remove a cube from the holder and insert it into the slot provided for it. The energy distribution to the drive, shields, weapons and shield balance can be adjusted using switches.

Compared to simulators like War Thunder, flying feels a little too slow, especially in the planet missions, but this is not noticeable in the heat of battle.

The game is definitely worth its money.

developer_response.body:
Skorpjon, I believe you might be slightly confused by the control scheme which differs slightly from games like Ultrawings. When you grab the flightstick, it tracks the rotation of the hand that is holding the flight stick, rather than the position. This makes it so you can rest the "virtual flightstick" comfortably in your lap. Give it a try! Grab the stick and rotate the controller rather than relying on positional tracking. Let me know if you have any trouble!

The first iteration of this game used positional tracking, but I switched it over to rotational tracking due to popular demand from players of a certain "VTOLVR" flight simulator game.

reviews.review_description:
From the moment you don the VR headset, you’re transported into a vivid, sprawling galaxy filled with danger and intrigue.
The controls are intuitive and responsive, but it does take some getting used to using the in game stick.  The controller thumb sticks do make it easier though, I found myself quite often having to go around in loops to get back on target.  Looking forward to playing some more to see what weapons and Starfighters will open up for me.
It’s quite immersive as you float around looking for the next battle with a great narrative that  ties everything together beautifully with the  story unfolding as you progress.
As an early access game whether you’re a VR veteran or new to the platform, Rogue Stargun is an unmissable experience.

Rogue Stargun VR – A Stellar Space Adventure

Rogue Stargun VR is an exhilarating space-themed shooter that blends fast-paced action with immersive VR gameplay. From the moment you put on your headset, you’re transported into the cockpit of a starfighter, armed with an impressive array of weapons and tasked with taking down waves of enemy ships across beautifully rendered cosmic battlegrounds.

Immersive Gameplay

The game excels in providing a seamless VR experience. The cockpit interface feels intuitive, and the controls are responsive, making every barrel roll, laser shot, and missile launch feel satisfying. Whether you’re dodging incoming fire or unleashing a barrage of lasers on an enemy fleet, the action keeps you fully engaged.

Stunning Visuals and Audio

The visuals are breathtaking, with vibrant galaxies, detailed starships, and explosive effects that light up your peripheral vision. The sound design complements the visuals perfectly, from the hum of your engines to the sharp crackle of enemy fire – it’s an auditory treat.

Progression and Customization

One of Rogue Stargun VR’s highlights is its progression system. As you advance, you unlock powerful weapons, shields, and upgrades, allowing you to tailor your starfighter to your playstyle.

Replayability and Challenge

The procedurally generated levels ensure every playthrough feels fresh. The difficulty scales well, offering a challenge to both newcomers and seasoned VR pilots.

Final Verdict

Rogue Stargun VR delivers a thrilling and polished space combat experience. With its engaging mechanics, stunning visuals, and replayable missions, it’s a must-play for fans of VR shooters and space adventures. Strap in, pilot – the stars await!
Good flying not so good knowing if your getting hit andnot a lot of life to collect makes the it flat as yh just get smashed

Simple graphics, but really good controls and fun gameplay. Missions are somewhat varied so far, and there is a simple story to accompany the dog fights. I like the other pilots popping up on the radio during the missions accompanied by the character portraits, and the scanner that identifies enemies and objectives.

If you want a space fighter sim, look no further, on the quest we have been starved of this kind of game .
Controls take some getting use to, but once you have mastered these you are a space fighter pilot, with 24 missions to conquer.
Never played any Star Wars fighter games so this is the next best thing.
Good work by the solo dev.
Top job.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the positive review! It is much appreciated, and this is very much a passion project!

reviews.review_description:
What a great game!!

Probably the best yet of it’s kind on the Quest!

Great graphics, great physics, immersive cockpit, various ships to control, good sounds!

Feels like a space shooter/sim that would play on pc or console. 
Closest thing yet to that experience and feeling like you are in a battle from Star Wars.

Battles in space and on the ground too!

Controls are good and have various options that you can change to have the controls suit your liking.

Highly recommend if you are a fan of space shooters/sims like myself!

reviews.review_title: Best game of this type handsdown!
reviews.review_description:
This game is awesome! What everyone else is saying. 

After devs mentioned to how the stick works everything kinda clicked. 

The tutorial is at your base as setting icons, click on that to get familiar with the ship. 

What got me was how the stick worked, most games like this the stick works as any flight controller but with this you have to move your wrist instead of pushing in the direction you want to go.  (I wished I could operate the stick normally instead of using my wrist.)

This game is a must get if you like flight sims. 


Devs- this is a perfect game for hand tracking to be implemented!

developer_response.body:
Have you tried rotating the virtual flightstick on the y-axis (up/down)?

Additionally the left thumbstick controls rudder/yaw as well under the default control scheme.

I think you may also be confusing pitch with yaw. If that's the case, you should be grabbing the virtual flightstick and rotating it after you grab. You can rest it on your lap after grab for stability. It should work like a joystick

reviews.review_description: After getting to grips with the controls the combat was really satisfying. The physical ship controls feel great, and loving that there’s planet based missions and not just outer space environments. The dialogue during missions really gave me Wing Commander vibes, in a good way!

If you want to experience the thrill of dogfighting in space, with a fantastic control scheme, exciting gameplay and nostalgic visuals as a native quest game; then this is the game for you. Immersive arcade action that comes highly recommended! Get this game and support this developer! Brilliant work.

reviews.review_title: Best Quest spaceship game I’ve played
reviews.review_description:
When I would daydream about the magical future of video games as a little kid, it was often being inside the cockpit of an X-wing, or Apollo 11. 

I’m an adult now, but ever since I first decided to buy the Quest 2 on a whim, I’ve bought basically every single game where you’re in the cockpit of a spaceship that has come out. Most of them have one of two issues: 

1) the ship is not interactive enough. if there are buttons, control panels, or other components, I want to be able to use all of them. When you see space shuttle videos and sci-fi movies, the pilots are always pushing satisfying-looking buttons and adjusting stuff. But there’s a catch:

2) When it comes to spaceship games, I HATE “survival arcade” gameplay. For some reason, in most of the other ones I’ve played, if there are a bunch of interactive components on board,  the game is primarily designed around them breaking, and you having to fix it, and this continues more and more until eventually you fail. How is that supposed to be fun? How is that supposed to feel even remotely realistic? How is that supposed to provide the sheer, childlike imaginative joy of *actually* being a spaceship pilot? It’s ridiculous! System management is great, but not as the primary gameplay gimmick. 

I want to feel like I’m actually flying the ship. 

And let me tell you: this game is the best example of that I’ve played yet. 

The graphics are functional, the missions are fun so far, the tutorial actually explains how to pilot the dang thing (!), all the buttons are interactive, and while stuff breaks (which it will), at least you’re not constantly inundated with annoyingly having to fix it as the primary gameplay device. 

It’s a great game. But moreover, I hope everyone buys it, so the developers will be given a lot more money to make a AAA version with cutting-edge graphics, ship components, missions, and all that. No kidding: devs, I would pay $40 for a big-budget blockbuster version of this with a movie-like narrative journey in a SECOND. 

Oh, and one more thing: I am horribly sensitive to motion sickness, to the point where even after several years I can’t play smooth locomotion games. A lot of flying games make me want to puke. But with this game? No motion sickness. None. It’s a miracle!!

Either way, bang for your buck wise, this might be the best sub-$15 game ever made. 9.6/10

Brings me back to X-Wing, TIE Fighter, and Wing Commander 3. Yes I’m dating myself. Simple and attractive visuals, solid flight feeling, and fun gameplay. Very approachable but still feels like a lite sim. We need more indie games like this in VR!

There's no other game I have found that runs on the Quest 2 that has this level of immersion. Most VR starfighter games just have VR bits tacked on where you still use a gamepad. Most flying games that can run on the Quest 2 have maybe 4-8 enemies max on the screen at any given time and every mission is just a repeat of the last one. This game takes the virtual cockpit idea you find in games like UltraWings to the next level and then throws you into massive fleet battles in a multi-hour single player campaign, all running smoothly on the Quest 2 and above. And the combat is varied with escort missions, assassinations, fleet combat, and bombing missions. There are dozens of powerups that completely change your ship weapons. Even Star Wars Squadrons cut land based missions from their game. In Rogue Stargun, by the final 3 levels, you will be zipping through canyons dodging enemy missiles while directing mechanized ground forces towards the enemy stronghold. There is no other VR experience like it on any platform!

If you like starfighter type games, you may well like this game. Lots of ships, power ups, and enemies.

The game starts off with getting you used to the mechanics, then it kicks off with big space battles. You can expect to get 8-10 hours of content at a fair asking price.

Solo dev with a full-time job has done well so far. He is constantly updating the game, and there is more to come. You can check my gameplay on Yoututube under @indievr

If you can say it about any VR game, this feels "old-school". First time in the cockpit reminded my of X-Wing and TIE-fighter on DOS/Windows. Great action, easy to understand controls. 

The whole experience just reminds me of space games of old, but now with full immersion in VR. I was a big fan of X-Wing vs TIE-Figher back in the day, and this keeps reminding me of the best bits of that. It runs well on my Quest 2. The graphics aren't UE5, but to me they look like they are SUPPOSED to look. It wouldn't look "right", if it was the latest fancy graphics. 

The UI is very easy to use. I figured it out in about two minutes without even realizing there were tutorials... That said, the tutorials themselves were very well put together. They get right into the point, and tell you exactly how you do what you need to do. You'll be flying in just a couple of minutes. 

The missions are short, so you'll never need to stay in-game for hours on end. It's easy to pick up and play a mission or two when you have time, then just put the game away. There's no 30min non-skippable tutorials without saving, etc. It's just a simple pick up a mission and shoot bad guys. I really liked that. 

The flight model is arcade and too sim-ish, if you know what I mean. It's actually refreshing to play a game that's meant primarily for having fun and shooting bad guys in a space ship, and less about micro-managing twelve thousand buttons. I mean I like that too, but that's a different experience. 

All in all, I will be returning to this game over and over again, which can't be said about many current games on the market.

I consider it very important to note that in many places I have read, according to the developer, Rogue Stargun is most closely related to the Ultrawings games - I am pleased to report that this is only partially true.

Ultrawings is one of the few games that really interested me (I was a huge fan of Stunt Island in the nineties and later of Pilotwings 64), but I never got beyond the downloaded Trial version because it made me very, very sick, even though I've been playing VR games for about 5 years and never had a similar problem before. Well, I'm happy to report: Rogue Stargun (which I was also very interested in, because I was a similarly big fan of the Wing Commander series and the X-Wing / Tie Fighter games) has absolutely NO resemblance to the Ultrawings series in this regard.

Rogue Stargun might only upset your image of VR space shooter games with its high entertainment value, but it won't upset your stomach. I've been waiting for a fully interactive cockpit since Star Wars Squadrons - here I got it, everything is clickable and you WILL click on everything, believe me.

The campaign is exciting and unprecedentedly diverse. I also have to praise the music: it brings out the feeling of "my ship, and I'm going to roast you" - you've never had a more "personal" spaceship. The secondary weapon system is also very cool.

Where should I stop? Rogue Stargun is a gem. If I were you, I wouldn't hesitate - forget that wishlist, buy it now!

This game is very promising need a few little tweaks but the dev knows that. 

For a solo dev that’s put this together on his own and to boot it not his “day job” wow he done a superb job! 

The cockpit is amazing really immersive, I’ve found myself getting a little lost a few times as I feel the navigation needs more visual pointers for the pilot other than that I’m loving it, graphics are great on the Quest 3 and can see hours of fun in this game. 

Well done one up for the Indie Devs 🙌

Beautiful shooter game. Understanding the movement is a little complex at first, but once you understand how to move it becomes very beautiful. I thought I suffered a lot from motion sickness, but it wasn't like that.

This is a good asking price for this and does good at looking pretty well for a voxel game. It does utilize the cockpit well and the asking price for the game is not much for what you're getting. I look forward to future updates.

An excellent start, with good overall mechanics that work with the medium.  Well worth tossing a few credits to the indie dev!

DEVELOPER UPDATE: thanks for taking pity on us poor atmo pilots who learned with control surfaces instead of directional thrusters!

b i t , l y / r o g u e s t a r v r

Very indie, but very fun! Would like more details one day... 🙃

It's a fun and cool little vr-space shooter. Varied missions, lots of ships and weapons and even some nice ship management! The graphics fits the game well. 

Well worth the price if you wanna fly around in space!
The ships handle well, the combat seems fun and there looks like a lot to do quest/mission wise.
Incredible that one guy made this. Runs really smooth and the awesome cockpit controls make you feel like you’re in your own starfighter. Can’t wait to see what else this guy cooks up!
I love this Game. Great smooth grafik, sound good, price really good and so much fun. GREAT for Early Access
